label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let me think through this problem step by step. So the question is asking whether following a series of movement instructions will bring me back to the starting point. The instructions are: always face forward, take 10 steps backward, then 7 steps backward, then 8 steps right, 6 steps right, and finally 3 steps left. The options are Yes or No. First, I need to clarify what "always face forward" means in this context. If I'm always facing forward, then when I take steps backward, I'm moving in the direction opposite to the direction I'm facing. So if I'm facing forward (let's say that's the positive y-axis in a coordinate system), then taking steps backward would move me in the negative y-axis direction. Now let's break down the steps: 1. Take 10 steps backward: since I'm facing forward, backward is the negative y direction. So this would be -10 on the y-axis. 2. Take 7 steps backward: again, negative y direction. This would be another -7 on the y-axis. So total y displacement is -10 - 7 = -17. 3. Take 8 steps right: if I'm facing forward (positive y-axis), then right would be the positive x direction. So +8 on the x-axis. 4. Take 6 steps right: again, positive x direction. So +6 on the x-axis. Total x displacement is +8 + 6 = +14. 5. Take 3 steps left: if right is positive x, then left is negative x. So -3 on the x-axis. Total x displacement becomes +14 - 3 = +11. So overall, after all these steps, my final position would be at (x = +11, y = -17) relative to the starting point. This is clearly not the same as the starting point (0, 0), so I wouldn't return to the starting point. Wait, but let me double-check. The question says "always face forward." Does that mean I keep facing the same direction throughout, or does facing forward change as I move? I think it means I keep facing the same direction throughout. So if I start facing forward (say north), then I don't turn at any point. So "right" and "left" would be relative to my facing direction.
